questions.text: Which connector is most common on modern Android smartphones for charging and data?
questions.explanation: USB-C is the standard reversible connector on current mobile devices.

questions.text: A technician must replace a damaged charging port on a flagship phone. Which port type is most likely required?
questions.explanation: USB-C is the standard reversible connector on current mobile devices.

questions.text: Which mobile feature uses short-range NFC for contactless payments?
questions.explanation: NFC enables proximity-based transactions on mobile wallets.

questions.text: Which iOS setting remotely locks and displays a message on a lost device?
questions.explanation: Lost Mode locks the device and can show owner contact info via iCloud.

questions.text: An iPhone is missing. Which feature lets IT lock it and show a recovery phone number?
questions.explanation: Lost Mode locks the device and can show owner contact info via iCloud.

questions.text: Which Android permission category controls access to the device camera?
questions.explanation: Camera permission gates photo/video capture apps.

questions.text: Which laptop feature converts a 2-in-1 into tablet mode via a rotating hinge?
questions.explanation: Convertibles use rotating or detachable hinges for tablet use.

questions.text: Which cellular generation introduced LTE-Advanced with higher throughput?
questions.explanation: 4G LTE brought broadband-class mobile data speeds.

questions.text: Users report slow mobile data on an older phone limited to EDGE. Which generation should they upgrade toward for LTE?
questions.explanation: 4G LTE brought broadband-class mobile data speeds.

questions.text: Which mobile OS component sandboxed apps to limit cross-app data access?
questions.explanation: Mobile OSes isolate apps in sandboxes for security.

questions.text: Which accessory extends laptop ports when the mobile device has limited I/O?
questions.explanation: Docks and hubs add HDMI, USB-A, Ethernet, and charging.

questions.text: A ultrabook has one USB-C port. Which accessory adds HDMI and wired Ethernet?
questions.explanation: Docks and hubs add HDMI, USB-A, Ethernet, and charging.

questions.text: Which screen technology offers deep blacks on many flagship phones?
questions.explanation: OLED pixels emit their own light, enabling true blacks.

questions.text: Which mobile troubleshooting step clears stuck network registration?

options.text: Toggle airplane mode or reseat SIM

questions.explanation: Airplane mode resets the cellular radio registration.

questions.text: A phone shows 'No Service' after travel. What is the first mobile-specific step?

options.text: Toggle airplane mode or reseat SIM

questions.explanation: Airplane mode resets the cellular radio registration.

questions.text: Which biometric method maps facial geometry on supported devices?
questions.explanation: Structured-light or IR facial mapping unlocks devices.

questions.text: Which tablet input accessory is pressure-sensitive for drawing?
questions.explanation: Active styluses support pressure and palm rejection.

questions.text: Which mobile deployment model installs a work profile separate from personal apps?
questions.explanation: Work profiles containerize corporate data on BYOD devices.

questions.text: Which connector did Apple Lightning replace on older iPhones?
questions.explanation: Lightning superseded the wide 30-pin connector.

questions.text: Which mobile battery best practice preserves longevity?

options.text: Avoid prolonged 100% charge and extreme heat
Fast-charge continuously overnight in hot car

questions.explanation: Heat and constant full charge accelerate lithium wear.

questions.text: Which wireless standard is typical for tethering a phone to a laptop?
questions.explanation: Personal hotspots share cellular data over Wi-Fi.

questions.text: Which TWO are common mobile device display issues? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Touch, pixel defects, and OLED burn-in are mobile display concerns.

questions.text: Which TCP port does HTTPS use by default?
questions.explanation: HTTPS encrypts HTTP traffic on port 443.

questions.text: A firewall rule must allow secure web browsing. Which port should be opened?
questions.explanation: HTTPS encrypts HTTP traffic on port 443.

questions.text: Which OSI layer handles logical addressing with IP?
questions.explanation: Layer 3 routes packets using IP addresses.

questions.text: Which cable category supports 1 Gbps to 100 meters on copper?
questions.explanation: Cat 5e/6/6a meet Gigabit Ethernet specs.

questions.text: Which device separates collision domains on each port?
questions.explanation: Switches forward frames per MAC address, isolating collisions.

questions.text: Which protocol dynamically assigns IPv4 addresses?
questions.explanation: DHCP leases IP, mask, gateway, and DNS to clients.

questions.text: Which wireless band offers more channels but shorter range than 2.4 GHz?
questions.explanation: 5 GHz has more non-overlapping channels at reduced range.

questions.text: Which DNS record maps a hostname to an IPv4 address?
questions.explanation: A records resolve names to IPv4 addresses.

questions.text: Which tool verifies Layer 1/Layer 2 link on an RJ-45 drop?
questions.explanation: Cable testers check continuity, pairs, and often wire map.

questions.text: Which IPv4 classless mask equals a /24 network?
questions.explanation: /24 leaves 8 host bits: 255.255.255.0.

questions.text: Which protocol synchronizes time across networked devices?
questions.explanation: NTP keeps clocks aligned for logs and certificates.

questions.text: Which connector is standard on twisted-pair Ethernet?
questions.explanation: RJ-45 is the 8P8C connector for Ethernet.

questions.text: Which address range is private RFC 1918 space?
Which wireless security mode provides per-user authentication on enterprise WLANs?
questions.explanation: 802.1X with RADIUS authenticates users individually.

questions.text: Which command on Windows displays the IP configuration?
questions.explanation: ipconfig /all shows IPv4, DNS, and MAC on Windows.

questions.text: Which fiber connector is common in data centers with a latch?
questions.explanation: LC is widely used on SFP/SFP+ transceivers.

questions.text: Which protocol resolves MAC addresses from known IPv4 addresses on a LAN?
questions.explanation: ARP maps IP to MAC on the local segment.

questions.text: Which TWO are valid IPv6 address types? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: IPv6 uses unicast, link-local, and ::1 loopback; no broadcast.

questions.text: Which component temporarily holds running programs and active data?
questions.explanation: RAM is volatile working memory for the CPU.

questions.text: Which CPU socket type must match the motherboard?
questions.explanation: CPU package must fit the board socket/chipset.

questions.text: Which storage interface offers NVMe speeds over PCIe?
questions.explanation: NVMe SSDs on M.2 use PCIe lanes.

questions.text: Which printer technology uses toner and a heated fuser?
questions.explanation: Laser printers fuse toner to paper with heat.

questions.text: Which power supply rating indicates higher efficiency at typical loads?
questions.explanation: 80 PLUS tiers reflect efficiency at 20/50/100% load.

questions.text: Which RAID level mirrors data across two disks?
questions.explanation: RAID 1 writes identical copies for redundancy.

questions.text: Which tool measures voltage, continuity, and resistance?
questions.explanation: Multimeters diagnose power and circuit issues.

questions.text: Which BIOS/UEFI setting boots from USB for OS installation?
questions.explanation: Boot order or one-time boot menu selects install media.

questions.text: Which GPU resource is most critical for 3D gaming performance?
questions.explanation: Graphics workloads depend on GPU and video memory.

questions.text: Which laptop component replacement requires matching wattage and connector?
questions.explanation: Adapters must meet voltage, amperage, and tip specs.

questions.text: Which ESD practice protects components during repair?

options.text: Work on carpet in dry winter

questions.explanation: Grounded straps dissipate static safely.

questions.text: Which connector powers most discrete GPUs directly from the PSU?
questions.explanation: High-watt GPUs need dedicated PCIe power connectors.

questions.text: Which memory type is error-correcting and common on servers?

options.text: Non-ECC SO-DIMM only in all cases

questions.explanation: ECC detects/corrects single-bit memory errors.

questions.text: Which symptom suggests failing capacitors on a motherboard?
questions.explanation: Bad caps cause instability, boot failures, or shutdowns.

questions.text: Which printer maintenance fixes streaks on laser output?
questions.explanation: Worn drums or low toner cause repeating streaks.

questions.text: Which form factor is smallest for mainstream desktop boards?
questions.explanation: Mini-ITX is the compact consumer standard.

questions.text: Which primary PC cooling assembly transfers heat from the CPU to air?

options.text: Only thermal paste without a heatsink

questions.explanation: Heatsinks spread heat; fans move air across fins.

questions.text: Which display interface commonly supports daisy-chaining multiple monitors?
questions.explanation: DisplayPort MST can chain compatible monitors.

questions.text: Which motherboard component manages communication between CPU, RAM, and PCIe devices?
questions.explanation: The chipset routes I/O and peripheral traffic.

questions.text: Which storage technology has no spinning platters?
questions.explanation: SSDs use flash memory with no mechanical spindles.

questions.text: Which TWO are hot-swappable in many server designs? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Drives, fans, and redundant PSUs often support hot swap.

questions.text: Which hypervisor runs directly on hardware without a host OS?
questions.explanation: Type 1 hypervisors (ESXi, Hyper-V host) sit on bare metal.

questions.text: Which cloud model provides ready-to-use applications over the internet?
questions.explanation: SaaS delivers apps like email or CRM as a service.

questions.text: Which virtual network device connects VMs to the physical LAN?
questions.explanation: vSwitches bridge VM NICs to physical uplinks.

questions.text: Which resource can be oversubscribed carefully in VDI pools?
questions.explanation: VDI hosts share CPU/RAM across many sessions with limits.

questions.text: Which Azure service category is IaaS?
questions.explanation: VMs are infrastructure you manage at the OS layer.

questions.text: Which feature snapshots a VM state for rollback?
questions.explanation: Snapshots capture disk and memory state points.

questions.text: Which container shares the host kernel unlike full VMs?

options.text: Full hardware VM always isolated kernel

questions.explanation: Containers share the OS kernel with isolation namespaces.

questions.text: Which cloud deployment mixes on-premises and public cloud?
questions.explanation: Hybrid connects private datacenter workloads to public cloud.

questions.text: Which AWS offering is object storage?
questions.explanation: S3 stores objects in buckets with high durability.

questions.text: Which virtual disk format is common on VMware?
questions.explanation: VMDK is VMware's virtual disk format.

questions.text: Which practice right-sizes cloud VMs to control cost?
questions.explanation: Rightsizing matches instance size to actual load.

questions.text: Which Google workspace tier is SaaS productivity?
questions.explanation: Workspace provides Gmail, Drive, and Docs as SaaS.

questions.text: Which hypervisor feature deduplicates identical memory pages across VMs?

options.text: Memory ballooning / transparent page sharing class features

questions.explanation: Hypervisors optimize RAM via sharing and balloon drivers.

questions.text: Which remote access protocol commonly accesses Windows VMs in cloud?
questions.explanation: RDP provides GUI remote desktop to Windows hosts.

questions.text: Which license concern applies when migrating Windows desktops to VDI?

options.text: Windows licensing for virtual desktops (VDI/SPLA rules)

questions.explanation: VDI requires appropriate Microsoft licensing.

questions.text: Which metric triggers auto-scaling of web servers in cloud?
questions.explanation: Autoscale adds/removes instances on load metrics.

questions.text: Which TWO are benefits of virtualization? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: VMs consolidate hardware, clone quickly, and isolate environments.

questions.text: A PC powers on but shows no video. Which step comes first?

options.text: Verify monitor input and cable seating

questions.explanation: Display path checks are quick and non-destructive.

questions.text: Intermittent Wi-Fi drops on one laptop only. Best next step?

options.text: Update Wi-Fi driver and check power management settings

questions.explanation: Client-specific issues often involve drivers or power save.

questions.text: A workstation cannot reach internal sites by name but can by IP. Likely cause?
questions.explanation: Name resolution failures point to DNS.

questions.text: Blue screen after new RAM install. First action?
questions.explanation: Improper seating or incompatible RAM causes crashes.

questions.text: Printer queue stuck with jobs. Quick fix on Windows?
questions.explanation: Spooler restart clears stuck print jobs.

questions.text: No POST beeps and no fans spin. Suspect?

options.text: PSU failure or front panel power connection

questions.explanation: No power delivery suggests PSU or power button wiring.

questions.text: Slow boot after many startup apps. Remediation?
questions.explanation: Startup bloat delays logon and boot time.

questions.text: Laptop overheats and throttles under load. Best maintenance?

options.text: Clean vents and replace dried thermal paste if needed

questions.explanation: Dust and paste degradation cause thermal throttling.

questions.text: Network link light off on one desk. First hardware check?

options.text: Test with known-good cable and switch port

questions.explanation: Swap testing isolates cable, NIC, or port faults.

questions.text: USB device not recognized on one port. Try?

options.text: Different port and Device Manager check
Flash BIOS before any port test

questions.explanation: Port, cable, or driver issues are common USB faults.

questions.text: Clicking hard drive and slow access. Likely issue?

options.text: Imminent drive failure — back up and replace

questions.explanation: Mechanical clicking indicates failing HDD.

questions.text: Mobile device rapid battery drain after update. Step?

options.text: Check battery usage report and reset network settings if radio stuck

questions.explanation: Updates can increase radio usage; usage stats identify culprits.

questions.text: Cannot join domain — time skew error. Fix?

options.text: Sync system time with domain controller/NTP

questions.explanation: Kerberos fails when client clock skew exceeds policy.

questions.text: Projector shows no signal from laptop. Check?

options.text: Correct input source and duplicate/extend display mode

questions.explanation: Input selection and display mirroring are common misses.

questions.text: Random shutdowns under gaming load only. Suspect?

options.text: PSU insufficient wattage or overheating GPU/CPU

questions.explanation: Peak power or thermal limits cause load-specific shutdowns.

questions.text: Workstation gets APIPA 169.254.x.x address. Cause?
questions.explanation: APIPA indicates no DHCP offer received.

questions.text: POST beep codes indicate RAM not detected. What should you try first?

options.text: Power off, reseat RAM, and test one module at a time

questions.explanation: RAM detection failures are often seating, slot, or module faults.

questions.text: After a patch panel change, an entire floor loses wired network. Best focus?

options.text: Verify cable pairs, switch port VLANs, and link lights

questions.explanation: Physical layer and switch port mapping errors affect many hosts at once.

questions.text: An inkjet printer shows color banding and streaks. First maintenance?
questions.explanation: Clogged nozzles and misalignment cause banding on inkjets.

questions.text: A replaced laptop touchscreen is inaccurate at the edges. Next step?

options.text: Calibrate the touch panel in OS settings

questions.explanation: Calibration maps touch coordinates to the display.

questions.text: Windows reports MEMORY_MANAGEMENT BSOD after a driver update. What helps isolate it?

options.text: Roll back the driver and test RAM with a memory diagnostic

questions.explanation: Bad drivers and faulty RAM both trigger memory management crashes.

questions.text: An external monitor flickers only when using a USB-C dock. Likely fix?

options.text: Update dock firmware and GPU/display drivers

questions.explanation: Docks and GPU drivers must support the display mode and bandwidth.

questions.text: A desktop wakes from sleep immediately at night without user input. Check?

options.text: Review wake timers, scheduled tasks, and peripheral wake settings

questions.explanation: Scheduled tasks, mice, keyboards, and network adapters can wake the system.

questions.text: Which TWO steps are safe general troubleshooting practices? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Structured troubleshooting changes one thing at a time and verifies outcomes.

acronyms.hint: Firmware that initializes hardware at boot
Modern firmware interface replacing legacy BIOS
Combines disks for performance or redundancy
Web traffic on port 80 by default
Central policy control for mobile endpoints
Cloud VMs and networks you manage
Backlight power for some laptop screens

acronyms.expansion: Small Outline Dual In-Line Memory Module
